_note: Light Ch 5 articles inline. Arts 500-506 (hazardous locations) and 517 (healthcare) have dedicated per-article files.

articles.scope: Repair garages where flammable liquids/gases are dispensed or transferred. Classified locations depend on whether the garage is major repair (Class I Div 2 within 18 in of floor) or minor repair (unclassified).

key_sections.title: Area Classification
key_sections.summary: Major repair: Class I Div 2 within 18 in of floor in service/repair areas. Minor repair: unclassified unless flammable fuel-dispensing or fuel-tank repair

key_sections.title: Wiring and Equipment Above Class I Locations
key_sections.summary: Above 18 in: ordinary location wiring acceptable; below 18 in: hazardous-location wiring per Art 501

common_errors: Treating an EV-repair garage as unclassified — EV battery rooms may warrant Class I Div 2 around electrolyte vent points
articles.divergence_notes: Art 511 + 500-506 use Class/Division (legacy US) and Class/Zone (Art 505); IEC uses Zone classification only. See hazardous-locations-classification.json for the conversion matrix.
articles.usage_notes: Always confirm the AHJ's expected classification — many jurisdictions adopt stricter local interpretations.

articles.scope: Hangars for aircraft maintenance/storage. Floor + 18 in classified Class I Div 2; 5 ft horizontal from aircraft Class I Div 2; full hangar Class I Div 2 above grade up to specific limits.

key_sections.title: Classification of Locations
key_sections.summary: Floor + 18 in: Class I Div 2 throughout. Within 5 ft of aircraft: Class I Div 2. Above 18 in but within the hangar volume: generally unclassified

common_errors: Failing to classify the wing-tip envelope when defueling — aircraft fuel ops temporarily extend the classified zone
articles.divergence_notes: Art 513 is highly application-specific; IEC handles via 60079-10-1 area classification with no aviation-specific guide.
articles.usage_notes: Coordinate with the hangar's fire-protection layout (foam suppression, fuel-handling SOPs).

articles.scope: Gas stations, EV-fast-charging-with-fuel-dispensing combo sites. Defines classified zones around dispensers (Class I Div 1 inside dispenser; Class I Div 2 within 18 in of grade × 20 ft horizontal).

key_sections.title: Class I Locations
key_sections.summary: Dispenser internal: Div 1. Within 18 in of grade × 20 ft horizontal radius: Div 2. Tank vents: Div 1 within 3 ft × Div 2 to 10 ft

key_sections.title: Seal-off requirements
key_sections.summary: Conduit seals at boundary of classified location

common_errors: Missing the 20-ft horizontal envelope around dispensers — frequent inspection failure
articles.divergence_notes: Art 514 dimensions (18 in, 20 ft, 3 ft, 10 ft) are NEC-specific; IEC uses similar zonal envelopes but in metric and via the API RP 500 / IEC 60079-10-1 source-based classification method.
articles.usage_notes: On a project that combines fuel dispensing + EV fast-charging, verify the EV equipment is sited OUTSIDE the Class I Div 2 envelope.

articles.scope: Spaces accommodating ≥100 persons (auditoriums, theatres, churches, conference centres). Wiring method restrictions, emergency-circuit independence.

key_sections.title: Wiring Methods
key_sections.summary: Metal-clad wiring (RMC/IMC/EMT/MC/AC) typical; NM cable prohibited in occupancies of >100 persons (exception: dwelling-style portions)

common_errors: Specifying NM cable in assembly occupancy floor — prohibited
articles.divergence_notes: NEC restricts NM cable in assembly occupancies; IEC has no NM-cable equivalent (uses singles in conduit/trunking by default).
articles.usage_notes: Confirm occupancy load classification with the building code (IBC) before selecting wiring methods.

articles.scope: Buildings housing livestock or storing/handling materials creating dust/corrosive/moisture issues. Equipotential bonding (547.10), site-specific wiring methods.

key_sections.title: Equipotential Planes and Bonding of Equipotential Planes
key_sections.summary: Equipotential plane required in animal-confinement areas — bonded to building grounding electrode system

common_errors: Omitting the equipotential plane in dairy/swine facilities — animals are sensitive to step voltages from stray currents
articles.divergence_notes: NEC's equipotential plane requirement (547.10) and IEC 60364-7-705 supplementary bonding rules cover similar ground but with different mandatory thresholds.
articles.usage_notes: Always include the equipotential plane on agricultural building designs.

articles.scope: Temporary wiring for construction, decorations, holidays. GFCI required on construction site receptacles (590.6).

key_sections.title: Ground-Fault Protection for Personnel (Construction Sites)
key_sections.summary: All 125 V/15-, 20-, 30-A receptacles on construction sites require GFCI protection (Class A) for personnel

common_errors: Using non-GFCI 'plug strips' on construction sites — prohibited; every receptacle must be GFCI-protected
articles.divergence_notes: NEC 590.6 GFCI requirement is functionally equivalent to IEC 60364-7-704 30 mA RCD requirement, but NEC requires Class A GFCI (4-6 mA trip) which is more sensitive than IEC general-use 30 mA RCD.
articles.usage_notes: Default to GFCI on all construction site receptacles regardless of voltage.

articles.scope: Class I/II/III, Division 1/2, Zone 0/1/2 classification systems and equipment requirements.
Patient care areas, Type 1/2/3 EES, isolated power systems, X-ray.
